name: React Router
description: The de-facto React router; v7 unifies the library and Remix's framework features

description.value: The de-facto React router; v8 unifies the routing library and Remix's framework features (loaders, actions, SSR) with always-on middleware.
description.comment: Evaluated at v8.2.0 (July 2026); v7 merged Remix's framework capabilities and v8 makes middleware and other v7 future flags the default.

repository.comment: Official GitHub repository under the remix-run org.

license.comment: SPDX MIT per GitHub license metadata and npm package manifest.

category.comment: Drop-in router you add to an existing React app; offers an optional framework mode but is not framework-bound, so per the guideline it gets the standalone-router tag.

active-maintenance.comment: Repo pushed 2026-07-14; v8.2.0 released 2026-07-08 — clearly active.

initial-release.comment: Repository created May 2014; first public releases that year.

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Weekly downloads of the primary `react-router` package for the week of 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-13. v8 dropped the separate react-router-dom shim, making `react-router` the primary package.

routing-paradigm.comment: Supports JSX <Route> declarations, createBrowserRouter route objects, and file-based routes in framework mode; navigation and route data via hooks (useNavigate, useLoaderData).

nested-layout-routes.comment: Nested routes with shared parent layouts via <Outlet> are a core feature.

dynamic-params.comment: Dynamic segments like /users/:id, read via useParams.

optional-catch-all-params.comment: Optional segments (:lang?) and splat/wildcard (*) routes are supported.

search-params-api.comment: useSearchParams hook provides a first-class read/write API for query string state.

programmatic-navigation.comment: useNavigate hook plus redirect() in loaders/actions.

route-guards-middleware.comment: Route-level middleware pipeline is always-on in v8 (the v7 future.v8_middleware flag was lifted and made default); every loader/action/middleware receives a RouterContextProvider.

written-in-typescript.comment: Authored in TypeScript with first-class type definitions.

type-safe-paths-links.comment: Framework mode generates route types and offers a type-safe href() helper so link/navigate targets are checked; requires the typegen step.

type-safe-params.comment: Route module type generation infers loader/action and params types per route (framework mode).

type-safe-search-params.comment: useSearchParams returns URLSearchParams (string values); no built-in schema-based typing/validation of search params, unlike TanStack Router.

codegen-required.comment: Full path/param type safety relies on the `react-router typegen` step (types emitted to .react-router/types) in framework mode; library mode is largely inference-only with less path checking.

type-safety-rating.comment: Typed paths (via href) and typed params/loaders through codegen, but search params remain untyped — matches the guideline's rating 3 (typed params/links with gaps).

route-data-loaders.comment: Route loaders with useLoaderData; available in both data (library) and framework modes.

actions-mutations.comment: Route actions and <Form>/useFetcher provide a built-in mutation primitive.

prefetching.comment: <Link prefetch="intent|render|viewport"> prefetches route code and data (framework mode).

pending-transition-ui.comment: useNavigation exposes pending navigation state; integrates with React transitions for pending/optimistic UI.

scroll-restoration.comment: <ScrollRestoration> component restores scroll position across navigations.

per-route-code-splitting.comment: route.lazy in data mode and automatic per-route module splitting in framework mode.

per-route-error-handling.comment: Per-route ErrorBoundary / errorElement scopes errors to a route subtree.

ssr-support.comment: Turnkey SSR in framework mode (Vite plugin + server runtime); library mode can also do manual SSR.

streaming-ssr.comment: Streaming SSR via deferred data and Suspense/Await (framework mode).

react-server-components.comment: First-class RSC APIs are documented for both Framework and Data modes, but as of v8.2.0 support is still marked unstable/opt-in (team plans to stabilize in a later minor).

static-site-generation.comment: Pre-rendering / prerender config in framework mode emits static HTML for chosen routes; SPA mode also supported.

client-only-usable.comment: Runs as a pure CSR SPA router in library/data mode or framework SPA mode with no server.

framework-bound.comment: Can be dropped into any React app as a library; the Vite-based framework mode is optional, so per the guideline it is not framework-bound.

bundle-size.comment: min+gzip of the full `react-router` package (8.2.0) per bundlephobia; this bundles the library plus framework runtime and is tree-shakeable, so a library-only SPA ships considerably less than this figure.

zero-runtime-dependencies.comment: Ships with a small runtime dependency (cookie); not dependency-free, though the footprint is minimal.

tree-shakeable.comment: ESM-only in v8 with side-effect-free modules, supporting dead-code elimination.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive docs: tutorials, library vs framework guides, how-to recipes, full API reference, and migration/upgrade guides — matches rating 5.

official-devtools.comment: No dedicated official route/navigation devtools inspector (unlike TanStack Router); debugging relies on standard React tooling.

data-fetching-integration.comment: Built-in loaders/actions are the primary data mechanism; documented (unstable) RSC/server-function integration in v8.

predecessor-lineage.value: Successor to Reach Router (merged back in); Remix's framework features were folded into React Router in v7, and v8 makes those defaults. Built on the history library.
predecessor-lineage.comment: Remix v2 framework capabilities live on inside React Router v7/v8 rather than as a separate product.
